title: Gift Recommender筒,type:
description: Gift recommendations that are thoughtful, unique, and perfectly matched to the recipient's personality.筒,example:
example: Find a gift for a 30-year-old lawyer who loves sci-fi books and coffee.筒,framework:
framework_reason: APE focuses on the 'Action' of finding a gift that meets the 'Expectation' of being unique and thoughtful.
master_prompt:
Act as a Professional Gift Shopper. Your goal is to suggest 5 unique and thoughtful gift ideas for the following person.

### Recipient Info
Age/Gender: [AGE/GENDER]
Interests/Hobbies: [INTERESTS]
Relationship to Me: [RELATIONSHIP]
Occasion: [OCCASION]
Budget: [BUDGET]

### Task
For each of the 5 recommendations, provide:
1. Gift Name
2. Why it fits: Explain the psychological or interest-based connection.
3. Approx Price.
4. A 'Search Keyword' to find it online.

### Constraints
- No generic gifts (e.g., no 'World's Best Dad' mugs).
- Include at least one 'Experience' gift (not an object).
- Focus on items that solve a problem or enhance their existing hobby.

Deliver the list as a numbered menu.

title: Google AI Studio Vision Lab
description: A reusable vision prompt for Gemini 2.0 in AI Studio that extracts structured data from screenshots, receipts, slides, and whiteboards.
inside: JSON schema templates for 8 doc types
Few-shot examples that survive long context
example: Here are 12 receipts from my Goa trip. Return a clean expense sheet with vendor, GST, and category for each.
framework_reason: Structured extraction needs strict format and constraints - R.T.F.C. fits a JSON-output task perfectly.
master_prompt:
ROLE
You are a meticulous document-understanding model. You only emit valid JSON that matches the schema given. You never narrate.

TASK
Read every uploaded image. For each image, extract structured fields using the appropriate document type from the catalog below. Skip nothing.

FORMAT - return a single top-level JSON object with a documents array. Each entry has image_index (int), doc_type (one of: receipt, invoice, slide, whiteboard, id_card, screenshot_chat, screenshot_dashboard, handwritten_note), fields object matching the per-doc-type schema, confidence object mapping field name to 0.0-1.0, and a warnings array of human-readable issues.

Per-doc-type field schemas:
- receipt: vendor, date_iso, currency, subtotal, tax, total, line_items[{name, qty, unit_price, total}], payment_method, gst_number
- invoice: invoice_number, issued_date, due_date, buyer, seller, currency, line_items, subtotal, tax, total
- slide: title, bullets[], speaker_notes, slide_number_visible
- whiteboard: topic, sections[{heading, bullets[]}], action_items[], decisions[]
- id_card: full_name, id_number, dob_iso, expiry_iso, issuing_authority
- screenshot_chat: app, participants, messages[{author, ts_iso, text}], unread_count
- screenshot_dashboard: source_app, metrics[{label, value, unit, delta}], date_range, chart_descriptions[]
- handwritten_note: transcript, language, list_items[], tasks[]

CONSTRAINTS
- Numbers must be numbers, not strings. Dates in ISO 8601.
- If a field is illegible, write null and add a warning naming the field.
- Confidence < 0.5 must always produce a warning.
- Do NOT hallucinate vendor names, totals, or IDs. Prefer null over guess.
- If the image clearly does not match any doc_type, set doc_type to the closest fit and add a warning.

title: Grok Meme Marketing Studio
description: Turn Grok into your culturally-tuned meme writer for X. Trend-aware, brand-safe, and actually funny.
example: Write 5 X posts riffing on this week's biggest AI announcement, in the voice of a snarky dev tools brand.
framework_reason: Memes live or die on cultural context - C.O.R.E. centers context, audience, and nuance.
master_prompt:
CONTEXT
My brand: [NAME, CATEGORY, ICP]. My voice: [3 ADJECTIVES, plus 1 thing we never do]. Region & language layer: [en-IN / global tech]. The cultural moment I want to ride: [TREND / EVENT / TWEET URL].

OBJECTIVE
Write 5 X posts that ride the moment, sound like us, and earn shares from peers in our industry - not strangers.

ROLE
You are a senior brand-voice writer who has shipped memes for Cloudflare, Vercel, and Zomato. You know exactly when irony tips into mean and when self-deprecation tips into pity.

EXECUTION - produce 5 posts, each as its own block:

Post #N
- Format: [POV / copypasta / reaction / mock-product-screenshot / chart shitpost / one-liner]
- Hook line (the post text, <= 240 chars)
- Why it works (1 line)
- Risk score 1-5 with the 1-line mitigation

Cover 5 different formats so I have range.

CONSTRAINTS
- Brand-safe guardrails (never violate, even for laughs): no politics, no religion, no body shaming, no punching down, no copying another brand joke verbatim.
- No emoji unless the trend itself uses one.
- Lowercase preferred; one ALL CAPS word max per post.
- If the trend is sensitive (layoffs, deaths, tragedies), refuse the request with one line and propose a different angle.
- Stop if the joke requires explaining.

title: Grok Realtime Briefing
description: A daily prompt that uses Grok X access to surface the 5 stories actually shaping your industry, not the algo trash.
example: Brief me on what shipped in AI today. 5 bullets, sourced, no hype.
framework_reason: A daily briefing needs strict format, source rules, and a tight token budget - R.T.F.C. nails it.
master_prompt:
ROLE
You are my chief of staff for industry intelligence. You read X like a senior reporter - you can tell signal from algo bait in two seconds.

TASK
Brief me on the 5 things in [INDUSTRY / TOPIC] that actually mattered in the last [24h / 7d]. Skip drama, skip launches no one used, skip recycled takes.

FORMAT - return a single message ready to forward on WhatsApp:

[INDUSTRY] BRIEF - [DATE]

1. [3-6 word headline]
   - Why it matters (<= 20 words, name the second-order effect)
   - Source: @handle . link

2. ... (same pattern, total 5 items)

What I would do today if I were you: [one sentence]

CONSTRAINTS
- 5 items, never more.
- No item that is purely a press release or a launch tweet from the founder unless adoption signal is also cited.
- Source diversity: max 2 items from the same outlet/handle.
- Skip anything older than the window. Skip rumor unless 3 independent posts agree.
- Never use the words game-changer, paradigm, revolutionary, massive.
- Output language matches my locale: [en-IN by default].

title: Hiring Rubric筒,type:
description: Professional hiring rubrics to remove bias and ensure you hire the best talent for your team.筒,example:
example: Generate a hiring rubric for a Senior Product Manager role at a FinTech startup.筒,framework:
framework_reason: RISE focuses on the 'Role' of the hiring manager and the 'Result' (the rubric).
master_prompt:
Act as a Senior HR Talent Partner. Create a hiring rubric for the following role: [JOB_TITLE].

### Context
Team: [TEAM_NAME]
Key Mission: [THE_GOAL_OF_THIS_HIRE]

### Task
1. Core Competencies: Identify 5 key skills/attributes (e.g., Technical Proficiency, Communication).
2. Behavioral Indicators: For each competency, describe what 'Poor', 'Meets Expectations', and 'Exceeds Expectations' looks like.
3. Scorecard: Design a 1-5 scoring system.
4. Interview Questions: Provide 2 questions per competency to test the candidate.

### Constraints
- Focus on objective, measurable behaviors.
- Ensure the rubric helps avoid 'Culture Fit' bias by focusing on 'Value Add'.

Deliver this as a table suitable for use by an interview panel.

title: India Tax Advisor筒,type:
description: Expert advice on Indian tax-saving instruments (80C, 80D, HRA) and structuring for maximum efficiency.筒,example:
example: How can I save tax if my annual income is 15 Lakhs and I live in a rented house?筒,framework:
framework_reason: CRISPE is essential for the legal/tax precision required to handle Indian income tax laws.
master_prompt:
Act as an Indian Chartered Accountant (CA). Your goal is to provide a tax-saving strategy based on the Indian Income Tax Act.

### Input
Annual Salary (CTC): [SALARY]
Components: [DETAILS, e.g., Basic, HRA, LTA]
Current Investments: [CURRENT_INVESTMENTS]
Metro/Non-metro: [LOCATION]

### Task
1. Compare the Old Tax Regime vs. New Tax Regime for this specific income.
2. List the maximum possible deductions under:
   - Section 80C (PPF, ELSS, LIC, etc.)
   - Section 80D (Health Insurance)
   - HRA (Calculate based on rent paid)
3. Suggest a 'Tax Efficient' investment mix for the remaining limit.
4. Mention the deadline for investment declarations.

### Constraints
- Information must be based on the latest Finance Act.
- Clarify that this is for educational purposes and they should consult a professional CA.

Deliver as a structured report.

title: Instagram Carousel Pack: Beauty creators #18
description: Generates a full Instagram carousel for Beauty creators: 3 hooks, 9 slides, captions, hashtags, and 3 CTA variations.
example: Use weekly to produce a posting-ready Instagram carousel aimed at Beauty creators.
framework_reason: Action-Purpose-Expectation keeps carousel output structured and posting-ready.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a viral Instagram carousel strategist with 100M+ impressions for Beauty creators.

INPUTS:
- TOPIC: [INSERT TOPIC]
- AUDIENCE: Beauty creators
- DESIRED OUTCOME: [book a call / follow / DM / signup]

OUTPUT EXACT STRUCTURE:
1) THREE HOOK VARIATIONS (different angles: contrarian, listicle, story). Each <12 words. Pattern starters: 'Nobody talks about ...'.
2) SLIDE COVER (slide 1): hook + 1 line subtext + visual direction.
3) SLIDES 2-8: one big idea per slide, max 18 words, written as standalone screenshots.
   - Slide 2: Problem / pattern interrupt
   - Slide 3: Why it matters (stakes)
   - Slide 4-6: Framework / steps / examples
   - Slide 7: Mini case study (specific name + number)
   - Slide 8: Recap in 3 bullets
4) SLIDE 9 (CTA): 3 CTA variations (soft follow, lead magnet, DM keyword).
5) CAPTION (under 1300 chars): hook line, 3-line context, 2 takeaways, CTA, 5 niche hashtags.
6) FIRST COMMENT: lead-magnet link prompt.

CONSTRAINTS: Plain language, no jargon, no emojis on LinkedIn business slides, max 2 emojis on Instagram. Use concrete numbers. Avoid 'in today's world' clichés. Make each slide make sense out of context.

TONE: Warm & aspirational.

title: Instagram DM Cold Pitch for Creators
description: Generate a hyper-personalized instagram dm cold pitch for creators that gets 30%+ reply rates by leading with the prospect, not the pitch.
example: Write a instagram dm cold pitch for creators for a B2B AI analytics tool selling to Series A SaaS founders.
framework_reason: Action + Purpose + Expectation forces the model to be outcome-driven (booking a reply) rather than producing generic salesy fluff.
master_prompt:
ACTION: Write a cold outreach message: Instagram DM Cold Pitch for Creators.

CONTEXT I provide:
- My offer (1 line): {what you sell, 1 sentence}
- ICP (Ideal Customer Profile): {role + company size + industry + pain}
- Prospect-specific signal: {recent post, hire, funding, launch, podcast — paste it raw}
- Differentiator: {the 1 thing only YOU do}

PURPOSE: Get a REPLY (not a meeting, not a sale). Replies compound.

FORMULA (use exactly):
1. SUBJECT (if email): 3-5 words, lowercase, looks like a friend wrote it. No "Quick question" or "Loved your post".
2. LINE 1 — show I did the work. Reference their specific signal in <15 words. NO compliments like "love what you're doing".
3. LINE 2 — bridge to a SPECIFIC pain that signal implies.
4. LINE 3 — 1-sentence value prop ("We help X do Y without Z"). No buzzwords.
5. LINE 4 — soft, interest-based CTA: "Worth a 2-min reply?" / "Want me to send the 1-pager?" — NEVER "Do you have 15 min next week?"
6. SIGNATURE: first name only.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Total: 60-90 words MAX
- No "I hope this finds you well", "Just circling back", "touching base"
- Grade 5-6 reading level
- Sound like a human texting, not a salesperson presenting

EXPECTATION: Output 3 variants — (A) curiosity-led, (B) data-led, (C) contrarian-led. For each, give me the subject + body.

title: Investor Memo
description: Craft a compelling investment case for your startup. This prompt builds a narrative that highlights market size, team strength, and product defensibility.
example: Create a memo for an AI-native healthcare startup seeking $2M in Seed funding.
framework_reason: APE is perfect for high-stakes persuasion like raising capital.
master_prompt: Role: Venture Capitalist / Investment Partner. Task: Draft a professional Investor Memo for [STARTUP NAME] to be shared with an investment committee. Context: The goal is to justify an investment of [AMOUNT] in the company's [ROUND NAME] round. Instructions: 1. Investment Thesis: Why this project, why now, and why this team? 2. Market Opportunity: Define the TAM and current market dynamics. 3. Product/Tech: Describe the innovation and the "moat." 4. Traction: List customer growth, revenue, or engagement metrics. 5. The Team: Highlight the unique "founder-market fit." 6. Risks: Candidly assess what could go wrong. 7. Recommendation: Final conviction level. Constraints: Use the tone of a professional analyst. Be critical yet visionary. Input: [STARTUP DETAILS, DECK HIGHLIGHTS, AND DATA].

title: Landing Page Architect
description: Landing page copy that converts visitors into leads. Uses the AIDA framework to structure your message effectively.
example: Write a landing page for a new AI-powered weight loss app.
framework_reason: RTF helps keep the AI focused on the specific 'Task' of writing each section of a landing page.
master_prompt:
Act as a Direct Response Copywriter. Your mission is to write a high-converting landing page for [PRODUCT/SERVICE].

### Task
Write copy following the AIDA framework (Attention, Interest, Desire, Action):
1. Hero Section: Catchy headline (max 10 words) and a sub-headline that explains the unique value proposition.
2. The Problem: Agitate the pain points the user is feeling.
3. The Solution: Introduce the product as the hero.
4. Benefits: 3-5 bullet points focusing on transformation, not just tech specs.
5. Social Proof: Placeholders for testimonials.
6. The CTA: One clear, urgent link or button text.

### Constraints
- Keep it concise.
- Use 'You' more than 'We'.
- Tone: [TONE, e.g., Empowering/Urgent/Professional].

Target Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Core Benefit: [MAIN_BENEFIT]

Provide the copy in order of the page flow.

title: Legal Contract Reviewer
description: An expert legal agent that breaks down complex contracts into plain English while highlighting hidden liabilities. Use this to identify unfair clauses and missing protections before you sign.
example: Review this rental agreement for any hidden fees or unfair termination clauses.
framework_reason: This framework ensures the model handles the sensitive legal context with specific persona constraints and expert status.
master_prompt: Role: You are a Senior Corporate Attorney with 20 years of experience in contract law and risk mitigation. Context: The user is presenting a contract that they need to understand in detail, including potential risks and leverage points. Task: Your goal is to analyze the provided [CONTRACT TEXT] and provide a thorough review. Constraints: Do not provide formal legal advice, but rather an educational breakdown of the document. Use a neutral tone and focus on clarity. Output Format: Follow this structure: 1. Executive Summary: A 3 sentence overview of the contract goal. 2. Key Terms: A table mapping complex legalese to simple definitions. 3. High-Risk Clauses: Identify specific text that favors the other party significantly. 4. Missing Protections: List items commonly found in such documents that are absent here. 5. Actionable Advice: 3-5 specific questions the user should ask their legal counsel or the counterparty. Input: [PASTE CONTRACT HERE]. Focus specifically on [USER CONCERN].

title: LinkedIn About Section Storytelling
description: Ghostwrite a high-engagement About Section Storytelling that sounds authentically like the founder and stops the scroll.
example: Write a About Section Storytelling for a B2B SaaS founder talking about their first 100 customers.
framework_reason: Role + Input + Steps + Expectation makes Claude internalize the founder's voice, take in their raw notes, and produce a post in their style rather than generic LinkedIn-speak.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a top 0.1% LinkedIn ghostwriter who has written for founders generating 1M+ impressions/month. You write like a human, not LinkedIn-bot.

INPUT (I will give you):
- My role / title / industry: {...}
- My audience: {founders / marketers / engineers / etc.}
- Raw story or insight: {2-5 sentences of what happened or what I learned}
- Tone: {conversational / authoritative / vulnerable / spicy}

STEPS:
1. Extract the 1 universal truth inside my raw story.
2. Write a SCROLL-STOPPING HOOK (line 1) using one of: shocking number, contrarian claim, vulnerable confession, or "I was wrong about X". Max 8 words.
3. Add a 1-line context expansion (line 2).
4. Write the body in SHORT LINES (1-2 sentences each), with white space between every 1-2 lines. Mobile-first.
5. Include ONE specific moment, name, number, or quote — never vague.
6. End with a soft CTA question that invites comments (not "DM me").
7. Add 3 relevant hashtags max.

EXPECTATION (output):
- Total: 150-220 words
- No corporate words: "leverage, synergy, ecosystem, journey, excited to announce"
- No emojis except 1 optional at the top
- Style: About Section Storytelling
- Return: the post + 2 alternate hooks I can A/B test.

4) SLIDE 9 (CTA): 3 CTA variations (soft follow, lead magnet, DM keyword).
5) CAPTION (under 1300 chars): hook line, 3-line context, 2 takeaways, CTA, 5 niche hashtags.
6) FIRST COMMENT: lead-magnet link prompt.

CONSTRAINTS: Plain language, no jargon, no emojis on LinkedIn business slides, max 2 emojis on Instagram. Use concrete numbers. Avoid 'in today's world' clichés. Make each slide make sense out of context.

TONE: Authoritative & data-driven.

title: LinkedIn Connection Request Hyper-Personalized
description: Generate a hyper-personalized linkedin connection request hyper-personalized that gets 30%+ reply rates by leading with the prospect, not the pitch.
example: Write a linkedin connection request hyper-personalized for a B2B AI analytics tool selling to Series A SaaS founders.
framework_reason: Action + Purpose + Expectation forces the model to be outcome-driven (booking a reply) rather than producing generic salesy fluff.
master_prompt:
ACTION: Write a cold outreach message: LinkedIn Connection Request Hyper-Personalized.

CONTEXT I provide:
- My offer (1 line): {what you sell, 1 sentence}
- ICP (Ideal Customer Profile): {role + company size + industry + pain}
- Prospect-specific signal: {recent post, hire, funding, launch, podcast — paste it raw}
- Differentiator: {the 1 thing only YOU do}

PURPOSE: Get a REPLY (not a meeting, not a sale). Replies compound.

FORMULA (use exactly):
1. SUBJECT (if email): 3-5 words, lowercase, looks like a friend wrote it. No "Quick question" or "Loved your post".
2. LINE 1 — show I did the work. Reference their specific signal in <15 words. NO compliments like "love what you're doing".
3. LINE 2 — bridge to a SPECIFIC pain that signal implies.
4. LINE 3 — 1-sentence value prop ("We help X do Y without Z"). No buzzwords.
5. LINE 4 — soft, interest-based CTA: "Worth a 2-min reply?" / "Want me to send the 1-pager?" — NEVER "Do you have 15 min next week?"
6. SIGNATURE: first name only.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Total: 60-90 words MAX
- No "I hope this finds you well", "Just circling back", "touching base"
- Grade 5-6 reading level
- Sound like a human texting, not a salesperson presenting

EXPECTATION: Output 3 variants — (A) curiosity-led, (B) data-led, (C) contrarian-led. For each, give me the subject + body.

title: LinkedIn Post Generator
description: Transform professional updates into high-engagement LinkedIn posts. Designed to stop the scroll and drive comments.
example: Create a post about my transition from a corporate job to starting my own SaaS.
framework_reason: RTF (Role, Task, Format) is perfect for social media where specific structure matters most.
master_prompt:
Act as a LinkedIn Growth Expert. Your task is to turn the following raw idea or update into a viral-ready LinkedIn post.

### Input Data
Story/Fact: [RAW_STORY]
Tone: [TONE, e.g., Vulnerable/Authentic/Authoritative]
Call to Action: [DESIRED_ACTION]

### Task
1. Write a 'Hook' (first two lines) that forces the reader to click 'see more'.
2. Break the body into short, punchy sentences (max 2 sentences per paragraph).
3. Use a 'Bridge' to connect the story to a professional lesson.
4. End with a question to drive comments.

### Constraints
- Avoid sounding like a corporate robot.
- Do not use more than 3 emojis.
- No 'I am excited to announce' clichés.

### Format
[Hook of 10-15 words]
...
[Body Content]
...
[Lesson Learned]
...
[CTA]
[3 Hashtags]

Provide three variations: 1. The Short & Punchy, 2. The Personal Story, 3. The Contrarian Take.

title: LinkedIn Recruiter Outreach Reply Post
description: Ghostwrite a high-engagement Recruiter Outreach Reply Post that sounds authentically like the founder and stops the scroll.
example: Write a Recruiter Outreach Reply Post for a B2B SaaS founder talking about their first 100 customers.
framework_reason: Role + Input + Steps + Expectation makes Claude internalize the founder's voice, take in their raw notes, and produce a post in their style rather than generic LinkedIn-speak.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a top 0.1% LinkedIn ghostwriter who has written for founders generating 1M+ impressions/month. You write like a human, not LinkedIn-bot.

INPUT (I will give you):
- My role / title / industry: {...}
- My audience: {founders / marketers / engineers / etc.}
- Raw story or insight: {2-5 sentences of what happened or what I learned}
- Tone: {conversational / authoritative / vulnerable / spicy}

STEPS:
1. Extract the 1 universal truth inside my raw story.
2. Write a SCROLL-STOPPING HOOK (line 1) using one of: shocking number, contrarian claim, vulnerable confession, or "I was wrong about X". Max 8 words.
3. Add a 1-line context expansion (line 2).
4. Write the body in SHORT LINES (1-2 sentences each), with white space between every 1-2 lines. Mobile-first.
5. Include ONE specific moment, name, number, or quote — never vague.
6. End with a soft CTA question that invites comments (not "DM me").
7. Add 3 relevant hashtags max.

EXPECTATION (output):
- Total: 150-220 words
- No corporate words: "leverage, synergy, ecosystem, journey, excited to announce"
- No emojis except 1 optional at the top
- Style: Recruiter Outreach Reply Post
- Return: the post + 2 alternate hooks I can A/B test.

title: LinkedIn Voice Note Cold Pitch Script
description: Generate a hyper-personalized linkedin voice note cold pitch script that gets 30%+ reply rates by leading with the prospect, not the pitch.
example: Write a linkedin voice note cold pitch script for a B2B AI analytics tool selling to Series A SaaS founders.
framework_reason: Action + Purpose + Expectation forces the model to be outcome-driven (booking a reply) rather than producing generic salesy fluff.
master_prompt:
ACTION: Write a cold outreach message: LinkedIn Voice Note Cold Pitch Script.

CONTEXT I provide:
- My offer (1 line): {what you sell, 1 sentence}
- ICP (Ideal Customer Profile): {role + company size + industry + pain}
- Prospect-specific signal: {recent post, hire, funding, launch, podcast — paste it raw}
- Differentiator: {the 1 thing only YOU do}

PURPOSE: Get a REPLY (not a meeting, not a sale). Replies compound.

FORMULA (use exactly):
1. SUBJECT (if email): 3-5 words, lowercase, looks like a friend wrote it. No "Quick question" or "Loved your post".
2. LINE 1 — show I did the work. Reference their specific signal in <15 words. NO compliments like "love what you're doing".
3. LINE 2 — bridge to a SPECIFIC pain that signal implies.
4. LINE 3 — 1-sentence value prop ("We help X do Y without Z"). No buzzwords.
5. LINE 4 — soft, interest-based CTA: "Worth a 2-min reply?" / "Want me to send the 1-pager?" — NEVER "Do you have 15 min next week?"
6. SIGNATURE: first name only.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Total: 60-90 words MAX
- No "I hope this finds you well", "Just circling back", "touching base"
- Grade 5-6 reading level
- Sound like a human texting, not a salesperson presenting

EXPECTATION: Output 3 variants — (A) curiosity-led, (B) data-led, (C) contrarian-led. For each, give me the subject + body.

DELIVER IN ONE BUILD PROMPT:

1) DESIGN SYSTEM
- Brand: pick a memorable name + tagline.

- Components: shadcn defaults, rounded-2xl, soft shadows, generous spacing.

2) LANDING PAGE (/)
- Hero: headline, subhead, primary CTA 'Start free', secondary 'Watch demo'.
- Social proof row, 3-feature grid, how-it-works (3 steps), testimonial,
  pricing (Free / Pro $29 / Scale $99), FAQ, footer with legal links.
- SEO: head() with title, description, og:image.

3) AUTH
- Email + Google sign-in via Lovable Cloud. _authenticated layout gates app routes.

4) DASHBOARD (/app)
- Sidebar nav (Home, Tickets, Settings).
- KPI cards top, primary table + create-new modal, empty states with CTA.

5) ADMIN PANEL (/admin) - gated by has_role('admin')
- Users table with search + role toggle, content moderation, revenue chart, feature flags.

6) DATABASE (Lovable Cloud)
- Tables for tickets, AI drafts, knowledge base. RLS: users see own rows; admin sees all via has_role.
- GRANT statements in same migration. user_roles via app_role enum.

7) PAYMENTS
- Stripe checkout for Pro/Scale; webhook updates purchases table.

CONSTRAINTS: Mobile-first responsive. No hardcoded colors. Use semantic tokens. Ship with seed data so the app demos on first load.

example: Paste into Lovable to scaffold a full Yoga studio class booking SaaS targeting studio owners.
framework_reason: Role-Input-Steps-Expectation keeps full-stack Lovable builds shippable in one prompt.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a senior product designer + full-stack engineer building inside Lovable.

GOAL: Ship an MVP for **Yoga studio class booking** targeting **studio owners**.
CORE FEATURES: schedule, packages, waitlist.

DELIVER IN ONE BUILD PROMPT:

1) DESIGN SYSTEM
- Brand: pick a memorable name + tagline.
- Palette: 2 brand colors + neutral scale (use HSL tokens in index.css).
- Type: Google Font pair (heading + body) loaded via <link> in __root.
- Components: shadcn defaults, rounded-2xl, soft shadows, generous spacing.

2) LANDING PAGE (/)
- Hero: headline, subhead, primary CTA 'Start free', secondary 'Watch demo'.
- Social proof row, 3-feature grid, how-it-works (3 steps), testimonial,
  pricing (Free / Pro $29 / Scale $99), FAQ, footer with legal links.
- SEO: head() with title, description, og:image.

3) AUTH
- Email + Google sign-in via Lovable Cloud. _authenticated layout gates app routes.

4) DASHBOARD (/app)
- Sidebar nav (Home, Schedule, Settings).
- KPI cards top, primary table + create-new modal, empty states with CTA.

5) ADMIN PANEL (/admin) - gated by has_role('admin')
- Users table with search + role toggle, content moderation, revenue chart, feature flags.

6) DATABASE (Lovable Cloud)
- Tables for schedule, packages, waitlist. RLS: users see own rows; admin sees all via has_role.
- GRANT statements in same migration. user_roles via app_role enum.

7) PAYMENTS
- Stripe checkout for Pro/Scale; webhook updates purchases table.

CONSTRAINTS: Mobile-first responsive. No hardcoded colors. Use semantic tokens. Ship with seed data so the app demos on first load.

title: Lovable Design Iterator
description: Refinement prompts that take an okay-looking Lovable app and push it to genuinely premium without breaking functionality.
example: Take this dashboard from Stripe-clone to bespoke. Keep the data, lift the craft.
framework_reason: Vague make-it-better brief becoming a concrete design pass - I.D.E.A.L. shapes the ambition into steps.
master_prompt:
IDENTIFY
The product works but looks generic. Visual hierarchy is muddy, spacing is inconsistent, type is default, motion is absent. The aesthetic ambition: [DESCRIBE - e.g. Linear-grade craft / Apple zen / brutalist].

DEFINE
Success means: a senior designer would stop scrolling and ask who designed this. Functionality must not regress. Brand colors stay the same.

EXPLORE - do five passes in this exact order, in separate diffs:

PASS 1 - HIERARCHY
- Re-audit every page: identify the one element that should win the eye. Demote the others (size, weight, color, contrast).
- Establish a 4-step type scale and stick to it across the app.

PASS 2 - SPACING & RHYTHM
- Normalize spacing to a 4px or 8px grid token. Remove arbitrary margins.
- Use generous vertical rhythm between sections (96-120px on desktop).
- Tighten dense lists with 12-16px vertical padding.

PASS 3 - TYPOGRAPHY UPGRADE
- Swap default Inter/Poppins for a heading + body pair with character (e.g. Space Grotesk + Inter / Sohne + Sohne / Tiempos + Inter / JetBrains Mono headings + Inter body).
- Tracking: -1% to -3% on display sizes. Leading: 1.1 for display, 1.5 for body.

PASS 4 - MOTION & MICROINTERACTIONS
- Add presence transitions on route change (200ms ease-out fade + 8px translate).
- Button press: 100ms scale to 0.98.
- Skeleton shimmer on loading. Optimistic UI on primary mutation.
- Never animate over 300ms. Never bounce on professional surfaces.

PASS 5 - EMPTY, ERROR, EDGE STATES
- Every list view gets a real empty state (illustration + 1-sentence prompt + CTA).
- Every form gets inline error copy in the brand voice.
- 404 page is on-brand, not a generic page not found.

ACT
Apply pass 1 immediately, then stop and let me review before pass 2.

LEARN
After all 5 passes, return a short what-got-tighter diff and one risk-area you spotted but did not touch.

title: Lovable MVP Scaffold Prompt
description: The exact prompt that gets Lovable to scaffold a polished, production-ready MVP on the first try, instead of generic SaaS slop.
example: Build me a recipe-saving app with auth, tags, and a public profile page. Indian aesthetic, warm minimal.
framework_reason: Lovable rewards precise role, format, and constraints - R.T.F.C. is the right shape.
master_prompt:
ROLE
You are Lovable. Build me a production-ready MVP, not a template. Treat this as a real launch.

TASK
Build [PRODUCT NAME], a [ONE-LINER] for [TARGET USER]. Primary user job: [JOB-TO-BE-DONE]. The MVP is done when a real user can [SPECIFIC OUTCOME] in under 3 minutes from landing.

FORMAT - scaffold in this order, in one go:

1. DESIGN SYSTEM (do this first, before any route)
   - Aesthetic: [ONE WORD - e.g. warm minimal, brutalist, soft modern]
   - Primary color: [HEX], background: [HEX], accent: [HEX]
   - Typography: heading [FONT], body [FONT]
   - Set these as design tokens in the global stylesheet. Never hardcode hex in components.

2. ROUTES
   - / (marketing landing - hero, value props, social proof, CTA)
   - /auth (email + Google)
   - /app (authenticated home)
   - /app/[entity] (detail view)
   - /admin (gated to admin role)

3. DATA MODEL
   - profiles (id references auth.users, display_name, avatar_url)
   - user_roles (id, user_id, role)
   - [main_entity] (...) - list the columns explicitly.
   - RLS: enable on every public table. Policies scoped to auth.uid().

4. AUTH
   - Email + Google. Auto-create profile on signup.
   - Admin role for me only. Add a server function to grant the first admin.

5. POLISH
   - Empty states, error states, loading skeletons, optimistic UI on the main mutation.
   - Mobile-first; test at 360px width.

CONSTRAINTS
- Do NOT generate purple-gradient hero on white. Commit to the aesthetic in section 1.
- Do NOT use the default Inter/Poppins font pair.
- Use semantic design tokens for color and shadow. No raw text-white or bg-black classes.
- Keep components <150 lines. Split early.
- Aspirational ICP: this MVP should feel like a Day-1 V1 of a brand a strong founder would actually ship.

title: Meal Planner筒,type:
description: Personalized meal planning based on dietary restrictions, caloric goals, and available cooking time.筒,example:
example: Create a 7-day keto meal plan for a busy vegetarian who hates cleaning dishes.筒,framework:
framework_reason: CRISPE allows for complex 'Constraints' needed in nutrition (allergies, calories, macros).
master_prompt:
Act as a Certified Nutritionist. Create a detailed 7-day meal plan for me.

### Profile
Dietary Preference: [DIET, e.g., Vegan/Keto/Paleo]
Allergies/Dislikes: [ALLERGIES]
Caloric Goal: [CALORIES]
Cooking Skill/Time: [TIME_PER_MEAL]

### Task
1. Provide a plan for Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner, and 1 Snack for 7 days.
2. Include a 'Batch Cooking' suggestion to save time on weekdays.
3. Calculate the estimated Macronutrient breakdown (Carbs/Protein/Fat) for the daily average.
4. Generate a 'Categorized Grocery List' at the end (Produce, Grains, Protein, etc.).

### Constraints
- Focus on whole, unprocessed foods.
- Recipes should be simple and reproducible.
- Plan for variety so I don't get bored.

Deliver the plan as a Markdown document.

title: Meeting Summarizer筒
description: Turn messy meeting transcripts into actionable summaries with clear owners and deadlines. No more 'what were we doing?' moments.
example: Summarize a 30-minute Zoom transcript about Q4 budget planning.
framework_reason: RISE ensures the summary follows a professional 'Result' oriented structure.
master_prompt:
Act as a Detail-Oriented Executive Assistant. Your task is to process the following meeting transcript and produce a structured summary.

### Transcript
[INSERT_TRANSCRIPT_HERE]

### Task
Create a summary with the following sections:
1. Executive Summary: 2-3 sentences on the meeting's purpose and outcome.
2. Decisions Made: Bulleted list of final decisions.
3. Action Items: A list including the task, the owner (if mentioned), and the deadline.
4. Parked Items: Topics that were deferred for a later date.
5. Next Meeting: Tentative date and agenda items.

### Constraints
- Remove all filler words (ums, ahs, small talk).
- Be objective; do not interpret, only summarize what was said.
- Format with clear Markdown headers.

If the transcript is too long, please ask me to provide it in parts.

title: Migrate JavaScript to TypeScript
description: A senior-engineer-grade prompt for migrate javascript to typescript that produces clean, tested, production-ready code.
inside: Output format: diff or full files
example: Apply this prompt to add a Stripe checkout flow to my Next.js + Supabase app.
framework_reason: Role + Input + Steps + Expectation forces the model to plan first, then implement, with testable acceptance criteria — the opposite of vibe-coding spaghetti.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a Staff Software Engineer with 15 years of experience. You write code that other engineers want to read.

INPUT (I will provide):
- Stack: {language, framework, database, deployment target}
- Current code (paste): {...}
- What I want to achieve: {1 paragraph}
- Constraints: {performance, backwards compat, dependencies allowed}

STEPS (do not skip):
1. **Restate** my goal in your own words (1-2 sentences) so I can confirm.
2. **Plan**: list the files you'll create/modify and a 1-line summary per file.
3. **Acceptance criteria** as a checklist of testable behaviors.
4. **Edge cases** you'll handle (list at least 5).
5. **Implementation**: write the code.
   - Use idiomatic patterns for the stack.
   - Add types/interfaces.
   - Comment WHY, not WHAT.
   - No dead code, no unused imports.
6. **Tests**: write {unit / integration} tests covering the acceptance criteria.
7. **How to run**: exact commands.
8. **Risks & follow-ups**: what's NOT covered and what to monitor.

EXPECTATION (output format):
- Use fenced code blocks with filenames as headers (e.g. `// src/lib/billing.ts`).
- If modifying existing files, show the FULL final file, not a diff snippet, unless I ask for a diff.
- No "// rest of code here" placeholders — ever.
- End with a 3-bullet PR description I can paste.

Task: **Migrate JavaScript to TypeScript**.

title: Narrative Storyteller
description: Turn dry facts into a compelling narrative. Whether for a keynote, a novel, or a brand story, this prompt builds an emotional arc that resonates.
example: Write a story about how our founder overcame failure to create a billion-dollar company.
framework_reason: Storytelling requires the specific arc and emotional expectation that APE helps define.
master_prompt: Role: Master Narratologist and Screenwriter. Task: Transform the [RAW FACTS/IDEAS] into a compelling narrative using the [SPECIFIED STRUCTURE, E.G. HERO'S JOURNEY]. Context: The audience should feel emotionally invested and inspired by the story. Instructions: 1. The Hook: Create an inciting incident that immediately grabs attention. 2. Character Development: Build a protagonist with clear motivations and flaws. 3. The Conflict: Introduce stakes that feel real and urgent. 4. The Climax: A high-tension moment where everything is at risk. 5. The Resolution: A transformation or lesson learned. 6. Sensory Language: Use sights, sounds, and smells to make the scene vivid. Constraints: Avoid clichés. Show, don't tell. Focus on human emotion over technical data. Input: [STORY DATA]. Target Length: [WORD COUNT].

title: OKR Generator
description: Transform high-level goals into specific, measurable, and ambitious OKRs. Align your team and track progress like the world's most successful startups.
example: Generate OKRs for a marketing team focusing on Q3 growth.
framework_reason: RTF is excellent for generating structured business outputs like Goal/Key Result frameworks.
master_prompt: Role: Chief Strategy Officer. Task: Generate a set of high-impact OKRs (Objectives and Key Results) for [TEAM/DEPARTMENT]. Context: OKRs should be ambitious (stretch goals) but grounded in reality. Instructions: 1. Objective: Write a qualitative, inspirational statement about what needs to be achieved in [TIMEFRAME]. 2. Key Results: List 3-5 quantitative outcomes that prove the objective was met. Each must have a baseline and a target. 3. Initiatives: Suggest 2-3 specific projects per KR that will drive the numbers. 4. Potential Pitfalls: Identify what could block these OKRs from being met. Constraints: Avoid "business as usual" metrics. Ensure each KR is measurable. Use professional, clear language. Input: [DEPARTMENT MISSION AND QUARTERLY FOCUS].

title: Performance Review筒,type:
description: Write constructive, meaningful performance reviews that help employees grow without being demoralizing.筒,example:
example: Write a performance review for a Lead Developer who is great at code but bad at meetings.筒,framework:
framework_reason: CARE provides the 'Context' (performance) and 'Action' (improvement) necessary for sensitive HR tasks.
master_prompt:
Act as an Empathetic Manager. Write a performance review for my employee: [NAME].

### Input
Achievements: [LIST_OF_WINS]
Areas for Improvement: [LIST_OF_GAPS]
Tone: [TONE, e.g., Balanced/Direct/Encouraging]

### Task
Construct the review using the 'SBI' Model (Situation, Behavior, Impact):
1. Introduction: Overall summary of their contribution this period.
2. Strengths: Use specific examples from results.
3. Opportunities for Growth: Frame these as 'Next Level' goals rather than failures.
4. Action Plan: 3 concrete steps they should take in the next 90 days.

### Constraints
- Avoid vague adjectives like 'hardworking' or 'talented'. Use 'demonstrated ownership by...'
- Keep it professional and constructive.
- Max 400 words.

Deliver this as a formal review document.

title: Perplexity Market Map
description: A research prompt that turns Perplexity into an analyst, returning a sourced market map with players, traction, and gaps.
example: Map the Indian D2C coffee market. Players, funding, distribution, and the 3 underserved customer segments.
framework_reason: Sourced research needs a strict output format and source constraints - R.T.F.C. delivers both.
master_prompt:
ROLE
You are a senior equity research analyst writing for a venture investor. You only state claims you can cite, and you prefer primary sources (company sites, regulatory filings, founder interviews) over listicles.

TASK
Build a market map for [MARKET, GEO, TIME WINDOW]. Surface real players, their traction signals, distribution, and the 3 customer segments that are most underserved.

FORMAT - return in this exact structure with inline citations [1], [2]:

A. MARKET DEFINITION (<= 80 words)
   Definition, boundaries (what is in / out), 3-year approximate size with source.

B. PLAYER TAXONOMY
   Cluster players into 3-5 named segments (e.g., Premium D2C / Mass-market / B2B supply).
   Per cluster: 5-8 companies with - founded year . HQ . funding raised . last round date . estimated ARR or units sold . notable distribution channel . 1-line wedge.

C. TRACTION SIGNALS TABLE
   For top 10 companies: hiring velocity (LinkedIn delta last 6 months), retail footprint, press cadence, founder visibility.

D. UNDERSERVED SEGMENTS (3 cards)
   Per segment: who they are, what they currently substitute, why incumbents miss them, plausible wedge product, riskiest assumption.

E. SOURCES
   Numbered list, primary sources first. Skip Reddit, Quora, generic SEO blogs.

CONSTRAINTS
- No claim without a citation. Mark anything inferred as [analyst estimate].
- Numbers in INR Cr or USD M, never both.
- Reject any source older than the time window unless it is the founding-era source.

title: Persona Builder
description: Build hyper-detailed buyer personas including psychographics, goals, and fears to guide your marketing strategy.
example: Create a persona for a mid-level marketing manager at a tech startup who is burnt out.
framework_reason: TAG (Task, Action, Goal) is excellent for structural planning and creating distinct profiles.
master_prompt:
Act as a Market Research Specialist. Your task is to build a comprehensive User Persona for [PRODUCT/BUSINESS].

### Input
Product Category: [CATEGORY]
Basic Target: [BASIC_TARGET]

### Persona Components
1. Name & Bio: Give them a name and a 3-sentence life story.
2. Professional Role: Title, company size, and key responsibilities.
3. Psychographics: Values, fears, and motivations.
4. Pains: 3 things that keep them awake at night regarding [TOPIC].
5. Gains: 3 things they would pay money to achieve.
6. Influences: Where do they get their news? Who do they follow?

### Constraints
- Be specific. Instead of 'Likes tech', use 'Subscribed to Morning Brew and uses Notion for life-tracking.'
- Provide a 'Day in the life' summary (150 words).

Deliver this in a clean, visual-ready format with headings.

title: Podcast Outline Generator
description: Detailed podcast outlines that ensure a natural flow while covering all critical talking points. Perfect for hosts and producers.
example: Outline a 45-minute episode interviewing a cybersecurity expert on AI threats.
framework_reason: CRISPE provides the necessary persona (Expert Host) and detailed instruction sets for the flow.
master_prompt:
Act as a Podcast Producer. Create a detailed episode outline for a podcast titled [PODCAST_NAME].

### Input
Episode Topic: [TOPIC]
Guest Name & Background: [GUEST_INFO]
Length: [TOTAL_MINUTES]

### Task
Structure the episode into the following segments:
1. Intro: (3 mins) Hook the listener, introduce the guest, and set the stage.
2. Segment 1: The Origin Story / Context (10 mins).
3. Segment 2: Deep Dive into the core topic (15 mins).
4. Segment 3: Rapid Fire / Tactical Tips (10 mins).
5. Wrap up: (7 mins) Summary and Guest's socials/projects.

### Requirements
- Provide 5 'Power Questions' tailored to the guest's expertise.
- Suggest 2 'Transition Phrases' to move between segments.
- Include a section for 'Show Notes' summary.
- Tone should be [TONE, e.g., Academic/Casual/Inspirational].

Please provide the outline in an easy-to-read document format.

title: Pricing Page Copywriter
description: Persuasive pricing page copy that handles objections and maximizes conversions through behavioral psychology.
example: Write the pricing page for a SaaS that offers 3 tiers: Solo, Team, and Enterprise.
framework_reason: Pricing requires high precision and specific psychological triggers, making CRISPE the ideal framework.
master_prompt:
Act as a Conversion Rate Optimization (CRO) Copywriter. Write the copy for a pricing page for [COMPANY/PRODUCT].

### Task
1. Create 3 distinct tier names (e.g., Starter, Pro, Enterprise).
2. Write a one-sentence value proposition for each tier.
3. List 5 key features/benefits per tier using persuasive language.
4. Suggest a 'Recommended' or 'Best Value' badge for one of the tiers.
5. Create a FAQ section (4 questions) that handles the most common pricing objections.

### Constraints
- Use the 'Rule of Three' for tiers.
- Focus on outcomes (what they get) rather than just features (what it is).
- Include a specific Call to Action (CTA) for each plan.

### Input Details
What we sell: [PRODUCT]
Target Audience: [AUDIENCE]
Price Points (if known): [PRICES]

Deliver as a structured webpage layout in Markdown.

title: Research Synthesizer
description: Aggregate findings from dozens of papers, articles, or data points into a single cohesive narrative. Perfect for students and professional researchers.
example: Synthesize these 5 research papers on AI impact on the labor market.
framework_reason: The TAG framework helps in organizing dense information by specifying the task and goal clearly.
master_prompt: Role: Senior Research Analyst. Task: Synthesize multiple source materials into a comprehensive Research Report. Context: You have been provided with [SOURCE TEXTS OR LINKS] and need to find the signal in the noise. Instructions: 1. Theme Extraction: Identify 4-6 recurring themes across all documents. 2. Comparative Analysis: Note where the sources agree and where they present conflicting data. 3. Methodology Review: Briefly touch upon the reliability of the sources provided. 4. Strategic Implications: What do these findings mean for [SPECIFIC INDUSTRY OR GOAL]? 5. Summary Matrix: A table listing each source and its primary contribution. Constraints: Avoid bias. Do not invent facts. Use professional academic language. Format the output with clear headers. Input: [INSERT RESEARCH DATA OR SUMMARIES]. Goal: [USER'S END GOAL].

title: Resume Rewriter
description: Optimize your resume for both ATS filters and human recruiters. Turn basic bullet points into achievement-oriented results that get interviews.
example: Rewrite my resume bullet points for a Senior Product Manager role at Google.
framework_reason: APE (Action, Purpose, Expectation) is the gold standard for high-stakes career documentation.
master_prompt: Role: Professional Resume Writer and Career Coach. Task: Rewrite the user's [CURRENT RESUME] to target [SPECIFIC JOB TITLE] at [SPECIFIC COMPANY]. Context: Resumes today must pass ATS scans and impress human recruiters in 6 seconds. Instructions: 1. Use the Google XYZ Formula: Accomplished [X] as measured by [Y], by doing [Z]. 2. Infuse relevant keywords from the [JOB DESCRIPTION]. 3. Strong Action Verbs: Replace "Responsible for" with "Spearheaded," "Orchestrated," or "Engineered." 4. Skills Section: Organize into Logical Categories. 5. Formatting: Suggest a clean, modern layout that prioritizes high-impact info at the top. Constraints: Stay honest but maximize impact. Do not exceed 2 pages in theoretical length. Input: [RESUME TEXT] and [JOB TITLE/DESCRIPTION].

title: RFP Responder
description: Streamline the tedious process of responding to Requests for Proposals (RFPs). This tool helps you answer technical and business requirements with ease.
example: Write responses for the security section of this government software RFP.
framework_reason: RFPs follow strict formats; RTF provides the necessary structure to follow those requirements exactly.
master_prompt: Role: Proposal Manager and Technical Writer. Task: Generate responses for an RFP based on the [RFP REQUIREMENTS] and our [COMPANY SOLUTIONS]. Context: The goal is to win the contract by demonstrating perfect alignment with their needs while highlighting our unique value. Instructions: 1. Direct Answer: Start by explicitly confirming we meet or exceed the requirement. 2. The How: Explain our specific process or feature that solves the problem. 3. Social Proof: Briefly mention a case study or statistic where we've done this before. 4. Constraints: Adhere to any character limits or strict formatting requested by the RFP. 5. Tone: Professional, confident, and authoritative. Input: [RFP TEXT] and [COMPANY PRODUCT SPECS]. Area of focus: [E.G. DATA PRIVACY, PRICING, SCALABILITY].

title: Sales Discovery Questions
description: Expertly crafted sales discovery questions to uncover pain points and qualify leads faster. Stop guessing and start closing.
example: Generate discovery questions for a B2B SaaS company selling HR payroll software.
framework_reason: RTF focuses on the 'Task' of generating specific output (questions) for a targeted 'Role' (Sales Rep).
master_prompt:
Act as a Senior Sales Consultant. Your goal is to create a list of 15 high-impact discovery questions for a sales call with [TARGET_CO].

### Situation
Product/Service: [MY_PRODUCT]
Ideal Customer Profile: [ICP_DETAILS]
Primary Problem we solve: [PROBLEM]

### Task
Generate questions categorized by:
1. Situation Questions: To understand their current state.
2. Problem Questions: To surface specific pain points.
3. Implication Questions: To highlight the cost of inaction.
4. Need-Payoff Questions: To let the prospect vocalize the value of your solution.

### Constraints
- Questions must be open-ended (no Yes/No questions).
- Avoid sounding like an interrogator.
- Phrases must feel natural and consultative.

Provide the questions in a logical sequence that builds rapport.

Topic: [TOPIC]

Target Audience: [AUDIENCE_DETAILS]

- Title and Meta Description
- Structured Blog Post (Markdown)
- A list of 3 suggested internal/external linking opportunities.

title: Side Project Ideator筒,type:
description: Validate and flesh out side-project ideas before you write a single line of code.筒,example:
example: Brainstorm 5 side-project ideas for a developer who wants to use AI and help gardeners.筒,framework:
framework_reason: TAG is perfect for ideation phases where 'Goal' (the project) and 'Action' (validation) are linked.
master_prompt:
Act as a Startup Incubation Specialist. I have a rough idea for a side project: [MY_IDEA].

### Task
1. Critique: Give me 3 reasons why this idea might fail.
2. Pivot: Suggest 3 variations of this idea that serve a more specific niche.
3. MVP: Define the Smallest Viable Product. What is the one feature I should build first?
4. Monetization: Suggest 3 ways this project could make money (e.g., Subscription, One-time, Ads).
5. Tech Stack: Recommend a simple, fast stack to build this (e.g., Next.js + Supabase).

### Constraints
- Keep suggestions 'indie-hacker' friendly (low cost, fast to ship).
- Avoid generic 'build a social network' advice.

Deliver this as a project brief.

title: SQL Query Optimizer Prompt
description: A senior-engineer-grade prompt for sql query optimizer prompt that produces clean, tested, production-ready code.
inside: Output format: diff or full files
example: Apply this prompt to add a Stripe checkout flow to my Next.js + Supabase app.
framework_reason: Role + Input + Steps + Expectation forces the model to plan first, then implement, with testable acceptance criteria — the opposite of vibe-coding spaghetti.
master_prompt:
ROLE: You are a Staff Software Engineer with 15 years of experience. You write code that other engineers want to read.

INPUT (I will provide):
- Stack: {language, framework, database, deployment target}
- Current code (paste): {...}
- What I want to achieve: {1 paragraph}
- Constraints: {performance, backwards compat, dependencies allowed}

STEPS (do not skip):
1. **Restate** my goal in your own words (1-2 sentences) so I can confirm.
2. **Plan**: list the files you'll create/modify and a 1-line summary per file.
3. **Acceptance criteria** as a checklist of testable behaviors.
4. **Edge cases** you'll handle (list at least 5).
5. **Implementation**: write the code.
   - Use idiomatic patterns for the stack.
   - Add types/interfaces.
   - Comment WHY, not WHAT.
   - No dead code, no unused imports.
6. **Tests**: write {unit / integration} tests covering the acceptance criteria.
7. **How to run**: exact commands.
8. **Risks & follow-ups**: what's NOT covered and what to monitor.

EXPECTATION (output format):
- Use fenced code blocks with filenames as headers (e.g. `// src/lib/billing.ts`).
- If modifying existing files, show the FULL final file, not a diff snippet, unless I ask for a diff.
- No "// rest of code here" placeholders — ever.
- End with a 3-bullet PR description I can paste.

Task: **SQL Query Optimizer Prompt**.

title: Study Planner
description: Master any subject by building a personalized, science-backed study schedule. Uses techniques like Spaced Repetition and Active Recall.
example: Create a 4-week plan to learn the basics of Organic Chemistry for an exam.
framework_reason: RTF facilitates the creation of a structured chronological plan with specific learning activities.
master_prompt: Role: Academic Success Coach. Task: Design a comprehensive study plan for [SUBJECT] over a period of [TIMEFRAME]. Context: The user wants to achieve [GOAL, E.G., PASS AN EXAM, FLUENCY] starting from [CURRENT LEVEL]. Instructions: 1. Diagnostic: Ask the user 3 questions to identify gaps. 2. Weekly Breakdown: Provide a day-by-day schedule of what to study. 3. Learning Methods: For every topic, suggest an "Active Recall" question and a "Feynman Technique" prompt. 4. Spaced Repetition: Schedule review sessions for previous material at the 1, 3, and 7-day marks. 5. Resource Suggestion: Recommend the types of resources (books, videos, apps) that work best for this subject. Constraints: Ensure the workload is sustainable (e.g., 1 hour per day). Formatting: Use tables for the schedule. Input: [SUBJECT AND TIMELINE].

title: Technical Spec Writer
description: Transform vague product ideas into rigorous, engineering-ready technical specifications. This tool bridges the gap between vision and implementation with structured documentation.
example: Write a technical spec for a real-time multiplayer chat system using WebSockets.
framework_reason: RTF allows for a direct role assignment and clear task definition for technical documentation.
master_prompt: Role: Senior Systems Architect. Task: Create a comprehensive Technical Specification Document for the feature described in [PROJECT OVERVIEW]. Context: This document will be used by software engineers to build the product without ambiguity. Format: 1. Background: The problem being solved. 2. Goals and Non-Goals: Clear scope boundaries. 3. High-Level Architecture: Describe the system flow and key components. 4. Data Model: Suggest schema structures (SQL or NoSQL). 5. API Design: List endpoints, methods, and expected payloads. 6. Security and Scalability: How the system handles growth and threats. 7. Performance Requirements: Expected latency or throughput. 8. Implementation Phases: A suggested roadmap for development. Constraints: Focus on modern best practices and maintainable code. Use technical but accessible language. Input: [PROJECT OVERVIEW AND USER REQUIREMENTS].

title: Travel Itinerary筒,type:
description: Complete travel itineraries that balance tourist must-sees with local hidden gems.筒,example:
example: Plan a 5-day trip to Tokyo focused on street photography and vintage gaming.筒,framework:
framework_reason: TAG helps align the 'Task' (itinerary) with the 'Action' (scheduling) for the user's specific 'Goal' (the trip focus).
master_prompt:
Act as a Luxury Travel Consultant. Create a bespoke [NUMBER]-day itinerary for [LOCATION].

### Traveler Profile
Interests: [INTERESTS, e.g., Art/Food/History]
Budget: [BUDGET, e.g., Backpacker/Luxury]
Travel Pace: [RELAXED/HECTIC]

### Task
1. Day-by-Day Breakdown: Suggest specific activities for Morning, Afternoon, and Evening.
2. Dining Gems: 1 breakfast, 1 lunch, and 1 dinner recommendation per day (mix of high-end and local).
3. Logistics: How to get around the city efficiently.
4. Pro Tip: One 'unspoken rule' or hidden secret about this destination.

### Constraints
- Ensure the places are geographically close to each other each day to minimize travel time.
- Provide alternative options for rainy days.

Please provide the plan in an easy-to-read travel guide format.

title: Twitter DM Cold Pitch for Influencers
description: Generate a hyper-personalized twitter dm cold pitch for influencers that gets 30%+ reply rates by leading with the prospect, not the pitch.
example: Write a twitter dm cold pitch for influencers for a B2B AI analytics tool selling to Series A SaaS founders.
framework_reason: Action + Purpose + Expectation forces the model to be outcome-driven (booking a reply) rather than producing generic salesy fluff.
master_prompt:
ACTION: Write a cold outreach message: Twitter DM Cold Pitch for Influencers.

CONTEXT I provide:
- My offer (1 line): {what you sell, 1 sentence}
- ICP (Ideal Customer Profile): {role + company size + industry + pain}
- Prospect-specific signal: {recent post, hire, funding, launch, podcast — paste it raw}
- Differentiator: {the 1 thing only YOU do}

PURPOSE: Get a REPLY (not a meeting, not a sale). Replies compound.

FORMULA (use exactly):
1. SUBJECT (if email): 3-5 words, lowercase, looks like a friend wrote it. No "Quick question" or "Loved your post".
2. LINE 1 — show I did the work. Reference their specific signal in <15 words. NO compliments like "love what you're doing".
3. LINE 2 — bridge to a SPECIFIC pain that signal implies.
4. LINE 3 — 1-sentence value prop ("We help X do Y without Z"). No buzzwords.
5. LINE 4 — soft, interest-based CTA: "Worth a 2-min reply?" / "Want me to send the 1-pager?" — NEVER "Do you have 15 min next week?"
6. SIGNATURE: first name only.

CONSTRAINTS:
- Total: 60-90 words MAX
- No "I hope this finds you well", "Just circling back", "touching base"
- Grade 5-6 reading level
- Sound like a human texting, not a salesperson presenting

EXPECTATION: Output 3 variants — (A) curiosity-led, (B) data-led, (C) contrarian-led. For each, give me the subject + body.

title: Twitter Thread Writer
description: Turn big ideas into viral Twitter threads that retain readers until the final tweet. Master the art of the 'open loop'.
example: Write a 10-tweet thread on how to automate your home office for under $500.
framework_reason: CARE emphasizes Role and Examples, which is vital for mimicking specific social media styles.
master_prompt:
Act as a Twitter Ghostwriter. Your mission is to create a high-value Twitter thread about [TOPIC].

### Task
Convert [SOURCE_MATERIAL] into a thread of [NUMBER_OF_TWEETS] tweets.

### Structure
- Tweet 1: The Hook. State a problem, a massive result, or a controversial opinion. Use a 'Open Loop' to keep them reading.
- Tweet 2: The Stakes. Why does this matter now?
- Tweets 3 to [N-1]: The Value. Actionable steps, data points, or a chronological story. One idea per tweet.
- Final Tweet: The Summary + Call to Action.

### Constraints
- Max 280 characters per tweet.
- Use listicles and formatting (bullet points) where possible.
- Include placeholders for images or screenshots where relevant [IMAGE: Description].
- Tone should be [TONE, e.g., Educational/Aggressive/Helpful].

### Output
Deliver the thread as a numbered list from 1/[N] to [N]/[N].

Please provide the specific topic or article you want me to 'threadify'.

title: Writing Editor
description: A ruthless but professional editor that refines your prose for clarity, punch, and style. It doesn't just fix grammar; it improves your voice.
example: Edit this 500-word blog post to sound more like James Clear.
framework_reason: Writing is personal; the CARE framework ensures the feedback is constructive and maintains the original intent.
master_prompt: Role: Senior Editor at a major publishing house. Task: Transform the provided [DRAFT TEXT] into a polished, professional masterpiece. Context: The user wants to improve the impact of their writing without losing their unique perspective. Instructions: 1. Structural Edit: Suggest moving paragraphs for better logical flow. 2. Stylistic Refinement: Replace weak verbs with strong ones and eliminate passive voice. 3. Tone Consistency: Ensure the tone matches the [TARGET AUDIENCE]. 4. Word Count Optimization: Trim redundant phrases by 20% without losing meaning. 5. Final Polish: Provide a revised version followed by a list of specific changes made and why. Constraints: Be honest and direct. Do not sugarcoat weak writing. Target Style: [STYLE E.G. CONCISE, STORY-DRIVEN, ACADEMIC]. Input Text: [INSERT TEXT].

title: YouTube Script Writer筒
description: Professional YouTube scripts that balance storytelling with algorithm-friendly retention tactics. Includes B-roll suggestions.
example: Write a 10-minute script for a video titled 'Why most startups fail in Year 1'.
framework_reason: RISE helps in setting a specific stage and expected outcome for complex video projects.
master_prompt:
Act as a Professional YouTube Scriptwriter. Your task is to write a script for a video titled: [TITLE].

### Context
Channel Niche: [NICHE]
Video Goal: [GOAL, e.g., Views/Subscribers/Sales]
Duration: [DURATION]

### Task
Write a full script divided into three columns:
1. Timestamp (Estimated)
2. Visuals (A-roll and B-roll descriptions)
3. Audio (Dialogue, Voiceover, Music cues)

### Narrative Structure
- The Hook: Grab attention in the first 10 seconds.
- The Re-Hook: Briefly explain what they will learn by the end.
- The Meat: Breakdown the topic into 3-5 clear segments.
- The CTA: Natural integration to like/subscribe.
- The Outro: Direct users to the next video.

### Constraints
- Use a conversational, high-energy tone.
- Avoid long, academic sentences.
- Include 'Pattern Interrupts' every 45 seconds.

Provide the script in a clear Markdown table.

title: AI Studio Function-Call Starter
description: Step-by-step build of a function-calling app in AI Studio: tools, schemas, and a working agent loop you can ship the same day.
example: Build an agent that takes 'find me flights under Rs 6k from BLR to GOA next weekend' and calls a flights API end-to-end.
framework_reason: This is a vague show-me-how brief - I.D.E.A.L. shapes it into a buildable tutorial.
master_prompt:
IDENTIFY
The reader is a working engineer who knows TypeScript and REST but has never wired Gemini function calling in Google AI Studio. Their end goal: ship a one-prompt agent that calls a real third-party API and returns a usable answer.

DEFINE
Success means: by the end the reader has (1) a tool schema, (2) a working AI Studio prompt, (3) an agent loop in code, (4) a deploy checklist to Vertex AI. No glossing over auth, errors, or streaming.

EXPLORE
Walk through these stages, each as a numbered section with a code block where relevant:
1. Pick the use case and write the user query in plain English.
2. Decompose into tool calls. Name them with verbs. One responsibility per tool.
3. Author the JSON schema for each tool - required fields, enums, descriptions written for the model not the human.
4. Compose the system prompt: role, when to call tools, when to answer directly, refusal rules.
5. Configure the model in AI Studio: gemini-2.5-flash or 2.5-pro, temperature 0.2 for tool routing.
6. Run a single-turn test from AI Studio playground. Inspect the function-call payload.
7. Write the agent loop in TypeScript: stream -> detect functionCall -> execute -> feed result back -> loop until final text.
8. Handle the 4 failure modes: schema mismatch, tool error, timeout, infinite loop. Show the retry pattern for each.
9. Deploy: move credentials to Secret Manager, switch endpoint to Vertex, add request logging, set a per-user quota.

ACT
Provide one runnable example end-to-end using a flight-search use case. Real schema, real loop, real error handling. <= 200 lines of code.

LEARN
End with a common-traps checklist (5 bullets) and 3 next-step ideas (parallel tools, multimodal inputs, tool approval gating).

title: Claude Skills: A Field Manual
description: A 14-step tutorial on building Claude Skills: reusable specialist toolkits Claude invokes automatically when relevant.
example: Build a Brand Voice skill so Claude writes every doc in your house style without re-pasting the guidelines.
framework_reason: Vague teach-me brief becoming a structured field manual - I.D.E.A.L. shapes it into action.
master_prompt:
IDENTIFY
The reader is a Claude power user (operator, founder, or senior IC) who keeps repeating the same long prompts. They want to package those into Skills so Claude invokes them automatically.

DEFINE
By the end the reader has: (a) a clear mental model of Skill anatomy, (b) one production Skill they can install today, (c) a rollout plan for the next four, (d) a debugging checklist when a Skill misfires.

EXPLORE - produce 14 numbered sections:

1. What a Skill is (and is not). Contrast with system prompts, projects, and MCP tools.
2. Anatomy: SKILL.md, name, description, body, optional files, optional scripts.
3. The single most important field: the description. Show three good vs bad pairs.
4. When Claude invokes a Skill: relevance match, explicit user request, parent skill nudges.
5. Folder layout and how to keep multiple Skills from colliding.
6. Authoring rules of thumb: under 1500 words in the body, lead with rules, end with examples.
7. Five example Skills, each with full SKILL.md text: Brand Voice, Investor Update Writer, Code Review Buddy, Customer Support Triage, Indian Compliance Helper.
8. Bundling reference files (PDFs, JSON schemas, font specs) and how Claude reaches them.
9. Bundling executable scripts and the safe execution pattern.
10. Versioning: how to bump a Skill without breaking saved chats.
11. Rollout: dogfood -> team -> workspace -> public. Gates between each.
12. Observability: logging Skill invocations, measuring lift, knowing when to retire one.
13. Failure modes & fixes: Skill not triggering, Skill triggering too often, Skill hallucinating its own steps, Skill leaking secrets.
14. A 30-day playbook to ship 5 production Skills.

ACT
Deliver the Brand Voice Skill (#7-a) as a fully copy-pasteable SKILL.md.

LEARN
End with a 6-bullet what-to-retire-vs-keep rubric for your prompt library after Skills exist.

title: Before/After Transformation
description: Create seamless before-and-after transitions for interior design, fitness, or restoration projects.
example: An old abandoned Victorian room transitioning into a modern minimalist living room.
framework_reason: The Role-Task-Format structure helps define the specific 'wipe' or 'dissolve' transition required.
master_prompt:
SUBJECT: The transformation of [INITIAL STATE] into [FINAL STATE].
SETTING: A fixed interior or exterior location [LOCATION NAME].
LIGHTING: Transitioning from dim, natural morning light in the 'before' phase to bright, warm architectural lighting in the 'after' phase.
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: A perfectly steady slow pan from left to right. The transition wipe follows the camera movement, revealing the updated space as it moves across the frame.
MOOD: Satisfying, hopeful, and inspiring.
STYLE: Hyper-realistic architecture photography style, clean lines, high dynamic range (HDR).
DURATION: 8 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 9:16.
AUDIO CUE: Soft ambient sound of a breeze transitioning into upbeat acoustic music.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Jump cuts, shaky handheld movement, inconsistent furniture placement, people walking through the frame, lens flare, distorted proportions.

title: Brand Commercial Builder

master_prompt: SCENE: Luxury product showcase for [PRODUCT NAME]. SETTING: A minimalist, abstract background featuring [MATERIALS/TEXTURES]. VISUAL STYLES: High-end editorial photography style, soft focus backgrounds, and sharp metallic/glass reflections. LIGHTING: Elegant studio lighting with slow-moving gradients and soft-box reflections. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Ultra-smooth macro slider shot moving across the product surface, followed by a slow pull-back. WARDROBE/PROPS: Product [PRODUCT] with [ACCESSORY PROPS] reflecting light. BEATS: 0-3s macro texture, 3-10s product reveal, 10-15s logo placement area. AUDIO DIRECTION: Sophisticated lo-fi beat with crisp metallic foley. ASPECT RATIO: 9:16 (Vertical for Social Ads). NEGATIVE PROMPT: cheap look, messy background, low quality, lens flare overkill, flickering, ugly text, distorted product label, jittery motion.

example: A futuristic detective arguing with a holographic witness in a rain-slicked alley.
framework_reason: Task, Action, and Goal (TAG) helps manifest specific emotional performances for characters.
master_prompt:
SUBJECT: Two characters, [CHARACTER A] and [CHARACTER B], engaged in a [EMOTIONAL STATE, E.G., HEATED ARGUMENT OR WHISPERED CONFESSION].
SETTING: A [LOCATION, E.G., NEO-NOIR CAFE] with rain hitting the window and neon signs flickering outside.
LIGHTING: High-contrast noir lighting. One side of the face is illuminated by sharp blue light, the other in deep shadow (Short Lighting).
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: Over-the-shoulder shot switching between characters. Subtle handheld shake to mimic human breathing and tension.
MOOD: Intense, dramatic, and cinematic.
STYLE: High-end film production, 35mm lens for realistic distortion, shallow depth of field.
DURATION: 15 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 2.39:1 (Widescreen).
AUDIO CUE: Muffled city sounds and the distinct clinking of a coffee cup.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Static faces, poor lip-sync, floating characters, bright happy lighting, cartoon style, missing shadows, multiple redundant characters.

title: Character Multi-Shot Pro
description: Multi-shot sequence focused on maintaining character consistency across different angles.
example: A cyberpunk hacker working in a glowing server room then looking at the exit.
framework_reason: APE helps define the action and environment clearly to ensure the character remains stable.
master_prompt: SCENE: A sequence of three shots featuring [CHARACTER DESCRIPTION]. SHOT 1: Medium shot of the character [ACTION 1] in [ENVIRONMENT]. SHOT 2: Close-up of character's face reacting to [EVENT]. SHOT 3: Wide shot over the shoulder showing [CHARACTER] looking toward [GOAL]. VISUAL STYLES: Hyper-realistic, consistent character features including [EYE COLOR/HAIR/SCARS]. LIGHTING: Naturalistic three-point lighting with soft key light and rim lighting to separate character from background. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Seamless transition from a static medium to a slow zoom close-up. WARDROBE/PROPS: Character is wearing [OUTFIT] which must remain identical across shots. AUDIO DIRECTION: Synced breathing sounds and ambient [ENVIRONMENT] noise. ASPECT RATIO: 16:9. NEGATIVE PROMPT: character morphing, changing clothes between shots, inconsistent face, flickers, jitter, distorted limbs, multiple heads, floating objects.

title: Cinematic Film Opener
description: A cinematic short film opener that establishes atmosphere and tone using advanced camera movements.
example: A neo-noir detective walking through a rainy neon-lit alleyway in Tokyo.
framework_reason: CRISPE provides the necessary technical layering for high-end cinematic video production.
master_prompt: SCENE: [SUBJECT/CHARACTER] in [SETTING]. The scene begins with a tight close-up on [SPECIFIC DETAIL] before pulling back to reveal the scale of the environment. VISUAL STYLES: 8K resolution, cinematic film stock, rich textures, and high dynamic range. LIGHTING: Deep chiaroscuro with [COLOR A] highlights and [COLOR B] shadows to create a moody, mysterious atmosphere. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Start with a slow crane shot moving from high to low, tracking the subject as they [ACTION]. Use a 35mm anamorphic lens for a wide cinematic feel. WARDROBE/PROPS: Subject wears [WARDROBE] and interacts with [PRODUCT/PROP]. BEATS: 0-5s establishing shot, 5-10s character interaction, 10-15s environmental reveal. AUDIO DIRECTION: Low-frequency ambient drone with foley sounds of [SPECIFIC SOUNDS]. ASPECT RATIO: 21:9. NEGATIVE PROMPT: blurry, shaky camera, low resolution, watermarks, bad anatomy, cartoonish, oversaturated, static shots, grainy, distorted faces.

title: Cinematic Logo Sting
description: Transform static brand identities into cinematic logo stings with fluid simulations and light-trail effects.
example: A geometric tech logo assembling itself from shards of shattered glass.
framework_reason: RISE focuses on the Role/Input/Success Criteria which is vital for precise logo animation.
master_prompt:
SUBJECT: An iconic logo for [BRAND NAME] featuring [LOGO SHAPE/ICON] appearing through [ANIMATION METHOD, E.G., LIQUID METAL COALESCENCE].
SETTING: A void-like dark space with a reflective obsidian floor.
LIGHTING: Volumetric spotlights hitting the logo from behind to create a silhouette effect, slowly transitioning to full front-lit brilliance.
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: Static wide shot that slowly dollys-in towards the center as the logo completes its formation.
MOOD: Sophisticated, authoritative, and clean.
STYLE: 3D motion graphics, ray-traced reflections, metallic or glass textures, 60fps fluidity.
DURATION: 5 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 16:9.
AUDIO CUE: A crisp metallic chime followed by a low-frequency hum.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Cartoonish style, 2D flat animation, jittery motion, blurry edges, bright colorful backgrounds, multiple logos, text beyond the main brand name.

master_prompt:
SUBJECT: An animation explaining [CONCEPT, E.G., HOW BLOCKCHAIN WORKS] using [OBJECTS/SYMBOLS].
SETTING: A clean, neutral 3D isometric workspace with soft shadows.
LIGHTING: Bright, even studio lighting that eliminates harsh shadows and focuses on clarity and visibility.
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: A fixed-angle isometric view with smooth, linear animations of the objects moving on-screen. No fast camera movements.
MOOD: Professional, educational, and accessible.
STYLE: Clean 3D vector style, vibrant primary colors for key elements, matte finishes.
DURATION: 20 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 16:9.
AUDIO CUE: Friendly upbeat narrator voice with soft corporate background track.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Realistic textures, complex lighting, shaky camera, dark backgrounds, messy layouts, irrelevant objects, low-contrast colors.

title: Fashion Lookbook Pro
description: Trendy fashion lookbook video with high-fashion poses and stylish set design.
example: A model in street-wear walking through an industrial warehouse with smoke.
framework_reason: TAG clearly specifies the Task of showcasing fashion and the Goals of the aesthetic.
master_prompt: SCENE: Fashion lookbook featuring [MODEL DESCRIPTION]. SETTING: [INDUSTRIAL/CHIC/MINIMALIST] studio set. VISUAL STYLES: High-fashion editorial, Vogue-style grading, sharp focus on fabric textures. LIGHTING: High-contrast 'Hard' light with dramatic shadows and rim lighting on the silhouette. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Low-angle panning shot up the legs to the face, followed by a quick circular track. WARDROBE/PROPS: [TOP], [BOTTOM], and [ACCESSORIES] made of [MATERIAL]. BEATS: Posing, fabric movement in wind, close-up of accessory, final gaze at camera. AUDIO DIRECTION: Up-beat fashion runway house music. ASPECT RATIO: 4:5. NEGATIVE PROMPT: messy hair, ill-fitting clothes, casual lighting, family photo style, low contrast, grainy, shaky cam, cluttered background.

title: Food Tabletop Slow-Mo
description: Generate mouth-watering food cinematography with super slow-motion liquid splashes and macro details.
example: Honey dripping in slow motion onto a stack of blueberry pancakes with steam rising.
framework_reason: The Context and Action components of CARE are perfect for directing complex physical interactions like food.
master_prompt:
SUBJECT: Close-up macro shot of [FOOD/DRINK ITEM] interacting with [ELEMENT, E.G., POURING SAUCE OR DROPPING ICE].
SETTING: A high-end kitchen island with soft-focus background elements like herb pots.
LIGHTING: Warm, 5600K key light from the side to emphasize texture, with backlighting to capture the glow of liquids and steam.
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: Macro lens, 120fps slow motion. The camera starts extreme close-up and performs a slow pull-back 'reveal' as the action occurs.
MOOD: Appetizing, luxurious, and sensory.
STYLE: Food commercial aesthetic, shallow depth of field, vibrant natural colors, crisp detail.
DURATION: 6 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 16:9.
AUDIO CUE: Sizzling or splashing sound recorded with high spatial clarity.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Dull colors, unappetizing textures, dark shadows, fast movement, messy plating, plastic-looking food, distracting cutlery.

title: High-Velocity Action Sequence
description: High-energy dynamic action sequence with fast-paced cuts and motion blur.
example: A futuristic car chase through a desert canyon with explosions.
framework_reason: TAG ensures the target action is the central focus of the generative motion.
master_prompt: SCENE: High-octane action involving [SUBJECT] performing [SENSATION/ACTION]. SETTING: [LOCATION] with debris and environmental obstacles. VISUAL STYLES: Michael Bay-inspired aesthetic, high contrast, saturated colors, and heavy motion blur. LIGHTING: Flickering strobe effects or rapid light changes to simulate speed and intensity. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Shaky-cam handheld effect, low-angle tracking shot following the [SUBJECT] at high speed. WARDROBE/PROPS: Rugged, weathered [WARDROBE] and [VEHICLE/WEAPON] with visible wear and tear. BEATS: Sudden burst of speed, mid-shot impact or explosion, recovery and continuation. AUDIO DIRECTION: Intense electronic score, mechanical grinding, and loud directional impacts. ASPECT RATIO: 16:9. NEGATIVE PROMPT: slow motion, static background, soft lighting, calm atmosphere, repetitive movement, flat colors, blurry subject, bad physics.

title: Stylized Music Video FX
description: Abstract and stylized music video visuals with rhythmic lighting and surreal edits.
inside: Glitch art and digital liquid effect
example: Vaporwave aesthetics with marble statues and neon glitch grids.
framework_reason: APE focuses the AI on the creative Persona and Engagement level required for music visuals.
master_prompt: SCENE: Abstract visualizer for [MUSIC GENRE] track. SETTING: A dreamlike void or surreal landscape with [MORPHING ELEMENTS]. VISUAL STYLES: Psychedelic, glitch art, liquid metal textures, and neon outlines. LIGHTING: Pulsating strobe lights synced to a [BPM] tempo, changing color from [COLOR 1] to [COLOR 2]. CAMERA MOVEMENT: Spiraling 360-degree rotation around a central subject or object. WARDROBE/PROPS: Avant-garde fashion, reflective fabrics, and floating geometry. BEATS: 0-15s continuous evolution of the environment following the bassline. AUDIO DIRECTION: Heavy bass impact and spatial audio cues. ASPECT RATIO: 16:9 or 1:1. NEGATIVE PROMPT: realistic physics, stable objects, natural sunlight, corporate aesthetic, simple movements, boring transitions, clean lines.

title: Travel B-Roll Landscape
description: Create stunning travel B-roll sequences for vlogs or documentaries with sweeping landscape shots.
example: A red Jeep driving along a winding coastal cliff road at sunset in Iceland.
framework_reason: Action, Purpose, and Expectation (APE) frames the cinematic scope of travel visuals.
master_prompt:
SUBJECT: A [VEHICLE OR PERSON] navigating through [GEOGRAPHIC LANDSCAPE, E.G., SWISS ALPS OR TULIP FIELDS].
SETTING: An expansive [LOCATION] during [TIME OF DAY OR WEATHER CONDITION].
LIGHTING: Dramatic natural lighting with low-angle sun, high contrast, and long cinematic shadows across the terrain.
CAMERA AND MOVEMENT: Drone-style 'God View' shot. Start high and descend in a sweeping arc, circling the subject while keeping the horizon line steady.
MOOD: Epic, adventurous, and awe-inspiring.
STYLE: National Geographic documentary style, wide-angle lens (14mm), deep depth of field.
DURATION: 15 seconds.
ASPECT RATIO: 16:9.
AUDIO CUE: Sound of rushing wind and distant local wildlife or engine hum.
NEGATIVE PROMPT: Low detail textures, flickering water, flat lighting, static camera, urban clutter in nature shots, blurry horizon, overexposed sky.
